# Robustness and within-person replication {#sec-robustness}

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8). Scaffold — an outline of intended content and its source artifacts, not finished prose.
:::

::: {.callout-important title="Causal status — [ROBUSTNESS]"}
Does the headline $\tau$ (@sec-itt) survive alternative assumptions, samples and specifications? These are checks *on* the causal result, not new effects.
:::

*[Scaffold]*

## Within-person replication: the waitlist-crossover DiD {#sec-robustness-did}

*[Scaffold]* The wait-list arm's own untreated → crossover transition, each child its own control (LRPDID01–05). **Triangulation, not independent replication** — it shares the untreated cell with the ITT. *Source:* `did_summary.csv`.

## General-ability adjustment {#sec-robustness-ability}

*[Scaffold]* Block-design robustness adjustment across the vocabulary family and reading anchors (LRPITT17–24).

## SES and matched comparators {#sec-robustness-ses}

*[Scaffold]* SES-adjusted and matched complete-case comparators (LRPITT13/13b/14/14b); the complete-case caveat.

## Dose (sensitivity only) {#sec-robustness-dose}

*[Scaffold]* A session-dose read flagged as sensitivity only — dose is a collider (@sec-dag-traps); observational, not causal.

## Prior sensitivity {#sec-robustness-priors}

*[Scaffold]* Re-fit under broader / alternative priors; report whether conclusions change. *Flag as future work where not yet run.*
